evals.name: version-recommendation
evals.expected_output: Analyzes conventional commits since the last tag and recommends a version bump. Presents commit counts by type and asks user to confirm or override.
assertions: Analyzes commits or conventional commit prefixes
Asks user to confirm or override the version

evals.name: explicit-version
evals.expected_output: Accepts version 0.5.0, normalizes to v0.5.0 tag, runs precondition checks.
assertions: Runs precondition checks (clean tree, CI, tag, draft, blockers)

evals.name: dry-run
evals.expected_output: Previews the release without creating a draft or dispatching the workflow.
assertions: Does not create a draft release or dispatch a workflow
Shows what would happen (version stamp, tag, dev bump)

evals.name: skill-only-fallback
evals.prompt: /release 1.0.0 (in a repo with no release workflow)
evals.expected_output: Detects no release workflow, creates the draft release, and prints manual tag/push instructions.
assertions: Scans .github/workflows/ and reports no release workflow found
Prints manual git tag and git push instructions

evals.name: first-release
evals.prompt: /release (in a repo with no prior tags)
evals.expected_output: Detects no previous release tag, asks user for version, offers empty or template editor for notes.
assertions: Asks user for version rather than recommending a bump
Handles note generation gracefully without a baseline

evals.name: precondition-failure-blockers
evals.expected_output: Identifies open blocker issues and stops before creating a draft.
assertions: Lists the specific blocker issues found
Stops execution and does not proceed to note generation
